title: Automate Facebook Comments in GoHighLevel — Lead Capture
description: Learn to build Facebook comment automation in GoHighLevel. Capture leads from comments, send automated DMs, deliver lead magnets, and build comment-to-capture funnels.
html_content:
Every day, thousands of potential customers comment on your Facebook posts—and most of them never hear from you again. That's lost revenue on autopilot.

Facebook comment automation in GoHighLevel changes that. Instead of manually responding to each comment, you can build intelligent workflows that instantly engage commenters, qualify leads, and deliver your best offers—all through messenger. I've helped agencies and businesses capture hundreds of leads this way, and the setup takes less than 30 minutes.

In this guide, I'll walk you through the exact workflow architecture that transforms casual Facebook engagement into a lead-capture machine. Whether you're running a service business, selling digital products, or managing client campaigns, this system works. And if you want to try it hands-on, GoHighLevel's free 30-day trial gives you double the standard trial period to experiment risk-free.

📋 In This Guide

  • Understanding Facebook Comment Automation in GoHighLevel
  • How to Set Up Your First Facebook Comment Trigger
  • Building Interactive Messenger Sequences for Lead Capture
  • Delivering Lead Magnets Through Automated DMs
  • Creating a Complete Comment-to-Capture Funnel
  • Advanced Automation: Keywords and Filtering

Understanding Facebook Comment Automation in GoHighLevel

Facebook comment automation is powered by workflows that listen for new comments on your posts in real time. When someone comments, the workflow triggers instantly—before they even know what happened. You can respond publicly on the post, send them a private message, or both.

The real power comes from what happens next. GoHighLevel can segment commenters based on their message content, send them personalized follow-up sequences through messenger, and move them directly into your sales pipeline. All of this happens automatically.

This works because people who comment are already engaged. They've seen your post, taken time to respond, and shown intent. The automation simply captures that intent and moves them closer to a conversion. In my experience, comment automation has a 2-3x higher engagement rate than cold outreach because the prospect initiated the contact.

💡 Pro Tip

The key to high conversion is speed. Your automated response should land within 60 seconds of the comment. GoHighLevel's instant triggers ensure commenters see your message before they even scroll away from the post.

How to Set Up Your First Facebook Comment Trigger

Setting up a Facebook comment trigger in GoHighLevel is straightforward. Here's the exact process:

Step 1: Connect Your Facebook Page

Navigate to your GoHighLevel account and go to Integrations. Find Facebook and click Connect. You'll authorize GoHighLevel to access your business page. Make sure you're connecting the correct page—agencies managing multiple client pages should repeat this step for each page.

Step 2: Create a New Workflow

Go to Workflows and click Create New. Choose Blank Workflow. Name it something clear like "Facebook Comment Lead Capture" so you can find it later.

Step 3: Add the Facebook Comment Trigger

Click Add Trigger. Search for "Facebook" and select "Facebook Comment on Post." You'll see options to filter by page. Select the specific page where you want this automation to run. You can also filter by post if you want to run the automation only on certain content.

Step 4: Add Your First Action

The trigger alone does nothing. You need to add an action. Click the plus icon below your trigger and add an "Update Contact" action. This converts the commenter into a contact in your GoHighLevel CRM—essential for tracking and follow-up.

Your first workflow is now live. Any new comment will automatically create a contact record. That's the foundation. Now we layer on the engagement.

Building Interactive Messenger Sequences for Lead Capture

A contact record is useful, but it doesn't move someone toward a sale. You need a messenger sequence that engages them at the moment they're most interested.

After the "Update Contact" action, add a "Send Message" action. GoHighLevel will send a Facebook messenger DM to the person who commented. This message should be personal, brief, and offer immediate value. Something like:

"Thanks for commenting! I see you're interested in [topic]. I just put together a quick checklist that breaks down the [specific problem]. Want me to send it over?"

This message does three things: thanks them, acknowledges their interest, and offers something valuable. It's a soft ask that almost always gets a positive response.

You can then add a delay (wait 5 minutes for example) and send a second message with the actual lead magnet or a link to a landing page. GoHighLevel supports messenger sequences up to 10+ messages, so you can build a full nurture flow from a single comment.

💡 Pro Tip

Use conditional logic in your sequences. If someone replies "yes" to your lead magnet offer, send them the asset immediately and follow up with a sales message. If they reply "no" or don't respond, move them to a nurture track instead of a sales sequence.

Delivering Lead Magnets Through Automated DMs

Lead magnets are the currency of digital marketing. They're valuable enough that people will trade their email or phone number to get them. Facebook comment automation is the perfect delivery mechanism.

You have two primary options in GoHighLevel:

Option 1: Send the Asset Directly in Messenger

If your lead magnet is a PDF, checklist, or template, you can attach it directly to a messenger message. GoHighLevel supports file attachments up to a certain size. The advantage: instant gratification. The disadvantage: you don't capture their email unless you ask separately.

Option 2: Send a Landing Page Link

Create a high-converting landing page in GoHighLevel (or link to an external one) and include it in your messenger sequence. When they click, they land on a page where they enter their email to download the lead magnet. This captures their contact info and automatically adds them to your email list.

I recommend Option 2 for most use cases. The extra step of entering their email is worth the contact data you capture. Plus, you can use that landing page across all your marketing channels.

Configure your automated message like this:

"Here's your checklist: [LANDING_PAGE_URL] — enter your email on the page and I'll send you the download link plus some extra tips."

That single message can convert 20-40% of commenters into email subscribers and qualified leads.

Creating a Complete Comment-to-Capture Funnel

A complete funnel has multiple stages: awareness, engagement, qualification, and conversion. Facebook comment automation handles the first three beautifully if structured correctly.

Stage 1: Instant Engagement (0-60 seconds)

Trigger fires, contact is created, first message lands in their messenger. This establishes that you're responsive and engaged.

Stage 2: Value Delivery (1-5 minutes later)

Second message offers and delivers your lead magnet. By now, they're checking messenger and see the follow-up.

Stage 3: Qualification (Day 1)

After they download, send a qualifying question: "What's your biggest challenge with [topic]?" Their answer tells you if they're a fit for your offer. Add their response as a note in their contact record for your sales team.

Stage 4: Soft Pitch (Day 2-3)

Based on their answer, send a personalized message that positions your service or product as the solution. Include a link to book a call, view pricing, or purchase.

This entire funnel runs on autopilot. One comment triggers a 3-4 day automated sequence that moves someone from stranger to qualified lead. Scale this across 10 posts per month and you're capturing 100+ leads per month with zero manual follow-up.

Advanced Automation: Keywords and Filtering

Not all comments are created equal. Some commenters are genuinely interested in your offer. Others are just making conversation. Advanced workflows use keywords and filtering to segment commenters into different sequences.

Keyword Triggers

You can set your workflow to fire only when a comment contains specific keywords. For example, if you're a fitness coach posting about weight loss, you might filter to trigger only when someone comments with words like "help," "struggling," "need," or "how." Those commenters get a sales sequence. Commenters saying "cool" or "great post" go to a nurture sequence instead.

Using Conditional Logic

In your workflow builder, add IF/THEN branches after the trigger. Example:

IF comment contains "help" or "struggling" → Send sales-focused sequence
IF comment contains any other text → Send relationship-building sequence

This ensures each prospect gets messaging aligned with their stated interest level. Higher intent comments get higher-pressure pitches. Low-intent comments get nurture content.

Page and Post Filtering

You might run different campaigns on different posts. Set your trigger to filter by specific posts or post types. A post about problem awareness gets a different follow-up sequence than a post about your pricing or results. This level of precision drives conversion rates up significantly.

Agencies managing multiple client pages should create separate workflows per client to maintain clean data and campaign control. GoHighLevel makes this simple—just duplicate a working workflow and change the page connection.
